title: Editas Medicine Q1 2026 Results: Narrowed Losses and EDIT-401 Progress
text:
Editas Medicine Q1 2026 Results Summary

Significant Loss Improvement:
Editas reported a Q1 2026 net loss of $25.0 million, or $0.26 per share, representing a 67% improvement from Q1 2025's net loss of $76.1 million or $0.92 per share. This demonstrates the impact of the company's restructuring efforts.

Operational Efficiency Gains:
R&D expense decreased to $17.6 million (down $9.0 million from prior year) primarily due to workforce reductions and discontinued clinical work on reni-cel. G&A expenses also declined by $3.1 million year-over-year, showing operational discipline.

Cash Position and Runway:
Cash position declined to $123.6 million as of March 31, 2026, down from $146.6 million at year-end 2025. The company projects cash will support operations into Q3 2027, providing adequate runway for EDIT-401 development milestones.

EDIT-401 Program Status:
The lead candidate continues to show promise with >90% mean LDL-C reduction in preclinical studies. The company remains on track to initiate first-in-human testing in 2026 with early proof-of-concept data expected by year-end.

Patent Victory:
The USPTO reaffirmed support for the Broad Institute regarding CRISPR/Cas9 patent rights exclusively licensed to Editas for human cell applications, removing a key uncertainty.
